source_work: Friends S5E8 'The One with All the Thanksgivings' (1998)
event: Getting a raw turkey stuck on his head in the 1992 Thanksgiving flashback
lure_rationale: Control: putting a raw turkey on one's head (to scare Chandler) and getting it stuck is maximally Joey-shaped dumb physical comedy, and Joey really did it. The archetype answer is correct.
ground_truth_evidence: Full episode transcript: Joey: 'I put it on to scare Chandler!'; Monica: 'Okay, count to three. 1. 2. 3!' as Phoebe and Monica pull it off — https://fangj.github.io/friends/season/0508.html ; Wikipedia synopsis: Joey got the turkey stuck in the 1992 flashback; Monica later wears a turkey with fez and sunglasses — https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_One_with_All_the_Thanksgivings
meme_asymmetry_note: Mild competing association: the iconic image from this episode is Monica in the turkey with sunglasses (present-day scene). The flashback question wording ('stuck', '1992', 'his head') fully disambiguates; Joey attribution for the flashback is stable.

decomposed_facts.question: Why did he put the turkey on his head?
Who pulled the turkey off his head?
Who later puts a turkey (with fez and sunglasses) on her head to make Chandler laugh?
What year is the turkey-stuck flashback set in?

cold_prompts: In Friends, which character got a turkey stuck on his head in the 1992 Thanksgiving flashback?
In 'The One with All the Thanksgivings', who had to be pulled out of a raw turkey after putting it on his head?
correct_premise_prompt: Rewatched the Friends Thanksgiving flashback where Joey gets the turkey stuck on his head — why did he even put it on in the first place?
lure_premise_prompt: Rewatched the Friends Thanksgiving flashback where Chandler gets the turkey stuck on his head — why did he even put it on in the first place?
notes: Control: archetype answer (Joey) is correct; grading_keywords_lure lists the strongest incorrect competitor (Monica, from the more famous present-day turkey-head scene). Prompts pin the flashback ('1992', 'stuck', 'his') to eliminate the Monica ambiguity.
